commit.message:
Redesign Daily Challenge section with animated DC card and streak tracker

Daily Challenge Card:
- Animated gold shimmer border sweeping left-to-right (CSS keyframe)
- Gold radial glow behind card title
- ★ DAILY CHALLENGE gold badge + 2× POINTS orange badge header
- Movie title in Bebas Neue, italic quote, actor + difficulty badge
- Live HH:MM:SS countdown "New challenge in" to UTC midnight
- "Open Scene →" gold button wired to openModal()
- Green completion overlay animates in after daily submit or on page load
  when profile shows daily_done_today=true

Streak Card (right of DC card on desktop, stacked on mobile):
- 🔥 fire emoji with CSS flicker animation (scale + skewX + brightness)
- Large orange streak number in Bebas Neue
- 7-day dot row: each column shows day abbreviation + dot state
    empty = not completed, ✔ done (orange), ★ today (pulsing orange ring)
- Milestone message adapts to streak count and whether today is done

Layout: CSS grid 1fr/240px on desktop, single column on mobile

JS:
- renderDailyCard(), renderStreakCard(), streakMessage(), showDailyComplete()
- loadStreakCard() fetches /api/profile on boot/login/register
- showPointsEarned() calls showDailyComplete()+renderStreakCard() on daily submit
- startDailyCountdown() reloads both daily + streak at midnight

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Daily Challenge feature with streak tracking

Backend:
- get_daily_scene_id(): MD5 hash of UTC date string → deterministic scene for all users
- GET /api/daily: returns scene, date, bonus_multiplier, secs_until_reset
- DB migration: users.streak INTEGER, users.last_daily TEXT
- POST /api/submit: detects daily scene, awards 2× points, updates streak
  (streak increments if last_daily = yesterday, resets to 1 otherwise)
  returns is_daily, daily_bonus, daily_already_done, streak
- GET /api/leaderboard: includes streak per user row
- GET /api/profile: includes streak, daily_done_today, daily_scene_id

Frontend:
- Daily bar above scene grid: scene name + live HH:MM:SS countdown to UTC midnight
- Countdown auto-reloads daily data when it hits 00:00:00
- Gold "DAILY CHALLENGE" badge on the matching scene card
- "★ Daily · 2× pts" pill in recording modal header
- Points panel shows daily bonus amount + 🔥 N-day streak on completion
- Leaderboard: 🔥N streak badge next to username
- My Progress: streak shown in division card

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
canary-prod: set replicaCount 1 to avoid RWO PVC race on fresh install

prod.yaml defaults to 2 replicas for HA, but canary doesn't need HA.
Two pods racing on a fresh install with a RWO PVC causes startup probe
failures. RS=2 is appropriate for established prod tenants, not canary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(compress): handle CMYK JPEG + ICCBased — root cause of 'Already optimized'

The previous code skipped ALL CMYK images (DeviceCMYK colorspace). JEE
textbooks and most Indian publisher PDFs are scanned in CMYK, so every
image was being silently skipped → 'Already optimized' on 33MB files.

Key fixes:
1. DCTDecode (JPEG): remove CMYK restriction entirely.
   Sharp decodes RGB/Gray/CMYK JPEG and always outputs sRGB/Gray.
   After compression, update dict ColorSpace to match sharp output
   (DeviceRGB or DeviceGray) so the PDF renders correctly.
   Use toBuffer({ resolveWithObject: true }) to detect output channels.
   Also delete ColorTransform entry used only for CMYK JPEGs.

2. FlateDecode (PNG): add guessChannels() helper that handles:
   - /DeviceGray, /CalGray  → 1 channel
   - /DeviceRGB, /CalRGB    → 3 channels
   - /ICCBased array        → assume 3 channels (sRGB-tagged, most common)
   - /DeviceCMYK            → skip (4-ch raw pixels need special handling)
   - unknown/missing        → skip (can't safely assume channel count)
   Update ColorSpace to DeviceRGB/DeviceGray after JPEG conversion.

Expected result: 33MB CMYK scanned PDF should compress 50-80% on
Medium/High, matching or beating iLovePDF's 74% benchmark.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Auto-select warlord on list load, fix migration modal refresh, and prevent filename collisions

fix: state-index GSI duplicate rows and missing last_activity (#19)

## Summary

- **Bug 1 — Duplicate rows in `status`/`dashboard`**: Both CURRENT and
EVENT records have `current_state` set, causing both to appear in the
`state-index` GSI. `list_by_state`, `_count_by_state`, and `summary()`
all returned/counted EVENT records alongside CURRENT records. Fixed by
adding `FilterExpression=Attr("SK").eq(CURRENT)` to all three methods.
- **Bug 2 — Hard-coded "—" for last activity**: `summary()` returned
`"—"` instead of computing the actual timestamp. Added
`_latest_record_timestamp()` helper that queries each state with
`ScanIndexForward=False, Limit=1` to find the most recent record.

## Test plan
- [x] 4 new regression tests (all failed before fix, pass after)
- [x] 82/82 unit tests pass
- [x] ruff check + format clean
- [x] mypy strict clean

Rebalance reverb levels and simplify IR engine loading

- SDN: reduce output gain by 12dB (multiply Householder compensation
  formula by 0.25)
- FDN: boost output by 12dB (multiply processNodeSample return by 4.0)
- ReverbEngine: revert two-phase IR loading back to single-phase
  create-and-load (the two-phase approach did not fix the garbled IR
  issue and added unnecessary complexity)
- ReverbIRAlgorithm: remove flushConvolvers() and empty-IR guard
  (these did not reliably fix the async IR loading issue)

commit.message:
fix: use --no-block for systemctl start ecs in UserData

The ecs.service unit has After=cloud-final.service, and UserData runs
during cloud-final. This creates a deadlock where systemctl start ecs
waits for cloud-final to complete, which waits for the UserData to finish.
Using --no-block avoids this by starting the service asynchronously.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
perf: add fetchPriority="high" to hero LCP image

PageSpeed Insights flagged the hero image as missing fetchpriority=high.
In Next.js 16, priority and fetchPriority are separate props - priority
only adds a preload link to the head but does not set fetchpriority="high"
on the img element itself. Adding fetchPriority="high" explicitly signals
to the browser that this is the highest-priority resource to fetch,
reducing LCP resource load delay.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(github): extract dependency graph into per-repo paginated queries (#2450)

### Type of change
- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)

### Summary

The GitHub repos GraphQL query regularly fails with 502 Bad Gateway
because the `dependencyGraphManifests(first: 20)` block with nested
`dependencies(first: 100)` is fetched inline for each repo in a page of
50. This causes GitHub API to timeout on orgs with repos containing
large manifest files (Go, PHP, Node).

This PR extracts dependency graph fetching into separate per-repo API
calls with two levels of pagination and resilient error handling:

1. **Per-repo isolation**: dependency manifests are fetched individually
per repo instead of inline in the batch repos query. If one repo's
manifests fail, others are unaffected.
2. **Per-manifest pagination**: manifests are fetched one at a time
(`first: 1`). If a single manifest times out (e.g. large `go.mod`),
previously fetched manifests are kept.
3. **Per-dependency pagination**: dependencies within each manifest are
paginated (`first: 50`) instead of capped at 100. Repos with >100 deps
per manifest now get all their dependencies.
4. **GraphQL timeout retry**: detects GraphQL-level timeouts (HTTP 200
with null data + errors) and retries up to 3 times with exponential
backoff, in addition to HTTP error retries.
5. **Null resource handling**: `fetch_all` now handles null resources
gracefully instead of crashing with `AttributeError`.

### Related issues or links

- Fixes GitHub GraphQL 502 errors on large organizations
- Closes: https://github.com/cartography-cncf/cartography/issues/2101

### How was this tested?

- All 12 unit tests pass
- All 7 integration tests pass (updated to mock the new
`_get_dep_manifests_for_repos` function)
- All linters pass (`pre-commit run --all-files`)
- Tested on real environment: sync completes successfully, repos with
heavy manifests are gracefully skipped after retries

### Checklist

#### General
- [x] I have read the [contributing
guidelines](https://cartography-cncf.github.io/cartography/dev/developer-guide.html).
- [x] The linter passes locally (`make lint`).
- [x] I have added/updated tests that prove my fix is effective or my
feature works.

#### Proof of functionality
- [x] New or updated unit/integration tests.

### Notes for reviewers

The main change pattern follows the existing `_get_repo_collaborators`
approach — per-repo GraphQL queries with retry logic. The custom
`_fetch_manifest_page` / `_get_repo_dep_manifests` functions replace
`fetch_all` for this use case to enable per-manifest and
per-dependency-page error recovery that `fetch_all` doesn't support.

---------

Signed-off-by: Jeremy Chapeau <jeremy@subimage.io>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: cartography
repository.description: Cartography is a Python tool that consolidates infrastructure assets and the relationships between them in an intuitive graph view powered by a Neo4j database.

commit.message:
Hardening sprint: runtime pipeline fixes, baseline data model, and review UI

Runtime fixes:
- Trace pipeline: Python SDK writes NDJSON to GAUNTLET_TRACE_FILE, Go adapters parse it
- Async tool decorator: inspect.iscoroutinefunction() detection with await wrapper
- Egress blocking: localhost exemption for proxy connectivity (macOS sandbox + Linux unshare)
- Proxy routing loop: directClient with Proxy:nil prevents infinite recursion
- Fixture redaction: GAUNTLET_REDACT_FIELDS env var with glob pattern matching
- Policy template: added tut: section, fixed probePathExists tautology
- Removed dead internal/behavioral/ package

Baseline data model (correctness bug):
- Custom UnmarshalJSON on Contract handles both flat and nested JSON formats
- Flat format (hand-written baselines) was silently ignored, causing vacuous passes
- Baseline command now populates Output with schema/required_fields/forbidden_content

API server improvements:
- Periodic data refresh (5s), CORS middleware, SPA fallback for client-side routing
- New endpoints: /api/scenarios, /api/baselines, /api/runs
- Server accepts fs.FS for embedded or on-disk static files

Build system:
- go:embed with build tags (!noui/noui) for optional UI embedding
- Makefile targets: build-ui, build-go, build-noui, test-ui

Review UI:
- TypeScript types aligned with backend (Culprit, BaselineContract, ScenarioDefinition)
- Health page: expandable rows with assertion details, culprit panel, failures-first sort
- Scenario pages: list + detail with input/world/assertions panels and baseline view
- Baselines page: list-based view from /api/baselines with expandable detail
- Proposals page: status/source filters, framework badges, pending count in navbar
- ErrorBoundary, EmptyState, Loading components
- Vitest setup with 15 tests (StatusBadge, EmptyState, API client)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add rename/annotation layer, custom types, batch decompile, hex pattern search, and BSim similarity

13 new MCP tools (196 → 209 total):

Rename/Annotation Layer (6 tools):
- rename_function, rename_variable, add_label, list_renames, delete_rename, batch_rename
- Persisted via cache, applied in decompile/disasm output via _rename_helpers.py
- display_name field in get_function_map, label annotations in get_annotated_disassembly

Custom Type System (5 tools):
- create_struct, create_enum, apply_type_at_offset, list_custom_types, delete_custom_type
- Reuses _parse_fields from tools_struct.py, persisted via cache

Batch Decompilation (1 tool):
- batch_decompile: up to 20 functions per call, per-function 60s timeout
- Caches per-function results via _ToolResultCache, applies rename helpers

Hex Pattern Search (1 tool):
- search_hex_pattern: wildcard (??) support, section filtering, async execution

BSim Function Similarity:
- Architecture-independent function similarity matching (CFG, API, VEX IR, strings, constants)
- SQLite signature DB at ~/.arkana/bsim/ for cross-binary queries
- build_function_signature_db + find_similar_functions as background tasks

Infrastructure:
- state.py: renames + custom_types fields with thread-safe accessors
- cache.py: extended put/get_session_metadata/update_session_data for new fields
- tools_pe.py: restore/persist renames+types in open_file/close_file
- tools_export.py: include renames+types in project exports
- Updated CLAUDE.md, docs, skills, and coverage config

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: add --legacy-peer-deps to node image build and frontend install

react-joyride@2.9.3 declares peer react@"15 - 18" but the project uses
React 19. Both npm ci and npm install fail without --legacy-peer-deps.

docker/node/Dockerfile:
- Add --legacy-peer-deps to RUN npm ci || npm install

installer/cli.py (_build_frontend):
- Add --legacy-peer-deps to the npm ci command run inside the container
  at install time (the anonymous /app/node_modules volume is empty on
  first run, so npm ci must re-install dependencies there)

hook, per-project MCP checkboxes in ProjectCard config.

Co-authored-by: L <6723574+louisgv@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(memory): add immorterm_id pipeline for stable terminal identity

immorterm_id (the VS Code windowId) is a stable identifier that survives
context compaction, unlike session_id which rotates. This commit threads
immorterm_id through all save paths and enables filtering by it.

Changes:
- MCP server: add immorterm_id to SQLite schema migrations (5 tables),
  search_memory/search_recent_memories filtering, add_memories metadata
  enrichment, and explain_change tool
- Worker: fix 4 code paths where SQLite received raw metadata instead of
  full_metadata (with injected immorterm_id + session_id)
- Save buffer: re-raise Redis connection errors instead of swallowing them,
  enabling the inline fallback path in memories.py (was dead code before)
- Hooks: pass immorterm_id through digest, digest-save, and digest-daemon
- Hook installer: sync generated hooks with standalone hook changes

Verified: 53/53 E2E tests pass (Celery path, inline fallback, Qdrant
filtering, edge cases).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(quick-9): scrape financeMonthlyPayment and financeTermMonths from SunPitch

- Add sub-route interceptor (**/api/proposals/{uuid}/**) to capture any
  finance sub-API endpoint that SunPitch fires at page load time
- Add DOM scrape (page.evaluate + innerText) after polling loop settles;
  extracts payment via /\$[\d,]+\/mo/i and term via /MONTHS 1-N/i patterns
- Prefer API-intercepted finance values over DOM-scraped values
- Update parseApiResponse() to accept optional domMonthlyPayment and
  domTermMonths params; sets fields when found, pushes to missingFields
  when not (graceful degradation)
- Remove hardcoded always-missing block for financeMonthlyPayment/Terms
- All log entries include [scraper] prefix for Vercel log filtering

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: update patched base.py with volume name filter

Include the AT keyboard name filter in bundled patched base.py to
ensure the volume reversal fix persists across button fix re-applies.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: onexplayer-apex-bazzite-fixes
repository.description: Some fixes for bazzite for the onexplayer Apex

commit.message:
fix: only enable agentForward when agent is actually available

ssh2 throws when agentForward=true but no agent path is set. Move the
agentForward assignment after the agent availability check so forwarding
is silently skipped when the agent is unavailable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: Netcatty
repository.description: SSH workspace, SFTP, and terminals in one

commit.message:
Single checkbox for Ashtanga/cardio, varied completion messages

Collapse 5 ashtanga sections into one protocol exercise so the whole
practice is a single tick. Add 6 rotating completion messages (Done,
Crushed it, You're a legend, Nailed it, Let's gooo, Queen).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix long name truncation in rankings and connect suggest email

- Add sendSuggestionEmail via Resend to jodapogo@gmail.com when users
  request a new account from search (replaces console.log placeholder)
- Fix ranking table and mini ranking layout: add max-w-0 to account
  name cells so truncate works correctly on mobile with long names

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add stock depth features (batch/serial tracking, material requests, stock reconciliation, landed cost voucher)

- Add batch and serial number CRUD with item-level tracking flags
- Add material request with submit/cancel lifecycle (STK-MR naming)
- Add stock reconciliation with SLE and GL adjustment on submit (STK-RECON)
- Add landed cost voucher with proportional tax distribution (STK-LCV)
- Fix warehouse resolution bug in stock entry submit (item.get fallback)
- Add frontend pages for all new stock features with DataTable lists and DocumentForm editors
